notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: ObjectStorageLink lifecycle exposes CREATING, ACTIVE, DELETING, DELETED, and FAILED. Repo-authored success is ACTIVE, requeue covers create-time CREATING plus delete-time DELETING, FAILED is terminal without requeue, and delete confirmation follows DELETED or NotFound.

Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateObjectStorageLink and UpdateObjectStorageLink return ObjectStorageLink bodies and do not expose opc-work-request-id headers, so the published runtime uses read-after-write observation for create and update rather than work-request polling.

Pinned OCI SDK contract: DeleteObjectStorageLink returns opc-work-request-id without a response body, so delete stays work-request-aware even though steady-state readiness is still classified from observed ObjectStorageLink lifecycle state.

Pinned OCI SDK contract: UpdateObjectStorageLinkDetails exposes only display_name, is_overwrite, freeform_tags, and defined_tags as in-place update fields. Fields absent from UpdateObjectStorageLinkDetails remain create-only drift, and ChangeObjectStorageLinkCompartment stays out of scope for the published runtime.

ListObjectStorageLinksRequest filters by availabilityDomain, compartmentId, displayName, id, lifecycleState, and lustreFileSystemId and returns Items []ObjectStorageLinkSummary. Safe reusable matching is exact compartment_id plus availability_domain plus lustre_file_system_id plus display_name, restricted to reusable ACTIVE or CREATING candidates; duplicate exact-name matches must fail instead of guessing.
